figures.caption: DeepSeek-R1-Zero's AIME 2024 accuracy (pass@1 and cons@16) and average response length steadily rising over the course of pure RL training, with accuracy surpassing the human-participant baseline.
figures.attribution: Figure from Guo et al. (2025), DeepSeek-R1, arXiv:2501.12948

figures.caption: Side-by-side schematic of PPO and GRPO: GRPO drops PPO's separately-trained value model, instead sampling a group of outputs per prompt and computing advantages from their normalized group-relative rewards.
figures.attribution: Figure from Shao et al. (2024), DeepSeekMath, arXiv:2402.03300

figures.caption: AIME 2024 accuracy of DAPO on Qwen2.5-32B rising with training steps (avg@32, pass@32, cons@32), surpassing the DeepSeek-R1-Zero-Qwen-32B baseline using about half the training steps.
figures.attribution: Figure from Yu et al. (2025), DAPO, arXiv:2503.14476

figures.caption: Overview of the Tulu 3 post-training recipe: core skill identification and data curation feed a multi-stage pipeline of SFT, DPO, and final RL with verifiable rewards, each stage paired with development and unseen evaluations.
figures.attribution: Figure from Lambert et al. (2024), Tulu 3, arXiv:2411.15124

figures.caption: Search-R1's PPO and GRPO training pipelines augmented with a search engine: a rollout module interleaves policy-LLM generation with search-engine calls, and the retrieved-plus-generated trajectory is optimized against the reward.
figures.attribution: Figure from Jin et al. (2025), Search-R1, arXiv:2503.09516

figures.caption: Comparison of text-based RL training against ReTool's RL training with interleaved code execution, where the policy LLM's rollout alternates between generated text/code and interpreter feedback from a code sandbox before reward and advantage computation.
figures.attribution: Figure from Feng et al. (2025), ReTool, arXiv:2504.11536

figures.caption: Overview of SWE-RL: a seed RL dataset is curated and selected from GitHub pull requests, then an LM is trained with GRPO using a reward that compares the model's predicted patch against the oracle patch (with a negative reward for malformed outputs).
figures.attribution: Figure from Wei et al. (2025), SWE-RL, arXiv:2502.18449

figures.caption: Overview of CodeRL's actor-critic framework for program synthesis: a pretrained LM is finetuned on problem-solution pairs, then an actor network is optimized by RL using a critic network trained to predict unit-test outcomes of sampled programs.
figures.attribution: Figure from Le et al. (2022), CodeRL, arXiv:2207.01780

figures.caption: Screenshot of the human-feedback interface used to collect process supervision: each step of a model's chain-of-thought solution is labeled positive, negative, or neutral (shown as red/neutral/green faces) to train a process reward model.
figures.attribution: Figure from Lightman et al. (2023), Let's Verify Step by Step, arXiv:2305.20050

figures.caption: Overview of rStar-Math: MCTS-driven deep thinking pairs a small language model with a process preference model to produce step-by-step verified reasoning trajectories, from which per-step preference pairs are constructed over four rounds of self-evolution.
figures.attribution: Figure from Guan et al. (2025), rStar-Math, arXiv:2501.04519

figures.caption: The Self-Rewarding Language Models loop: a model generates new prompts and candidate responses, acts as its own LLM-as-a-Judge to score them into preference pairs, and is retrained via DPO into the next-iteration model.
figures.attribution: Figure from Yuan et al. (2024), Self-Rewarding Language Models, arXiv:2401.10020

figures.caption: Illustration of generative verifiers: GenRM scores a solution by the token probability of answering 'Yes' to a correctness question, while GenRM-CoT samples multiple verification chains-of-thought and averages their Yes/No verdicts into the reward.
figures.attribution: Figure from Zhang et al. (2024), Generative Verifiers, arXiv:2408.15240

figures.caption: Overview of Rubrics as Rewards (RaR): an LLM with human-expert guidance generates prompt-specific rubric criteria, which an LLM judge uses to score policy-model rollouts into a rubric reward that drives GRPO training.
figures.attribution: Figure from Gunjal et al. (2025), Rubrics as Rewards, arXiv:2507.17746

figures.caption: Framework of Visual-RFT: a policy vision-language model generates reasoning-plus-answer responses to an image question, which are scored by verifiable rewards (IoU reward for detection, classification reward) and used for policy-gradient optimization against a frozen reference model.
figures.attribution: Figure from Liu et al. (2025), Visual-RFT, arXiv:2503.01785

figures.caption: Two-stage pipeline for HuatuoGPT-o1 medical reasoning: Stage 1 uses a verifier and search strategies to build complex chain-of-thought data for SFT, then Stage 2 enhances reasoning with verifiable-reward RL (PPO) on medical problems.
figures.attribution: Figure from Chen et al. (2024), HuatuoGPT-o1, arXiv:2412.18925

figures.caption: Illustration that RLVR narrows rather than expands reasoning capacity: sampling trees show RLVR yields more efficient sampling of correct paths but reduced overall coverage, and pass@k curves cross so the base model overtakes RLVR-trained models at large k.
figures.attribution: Figure from Yue et al. (2025), Does RL Really Incentivize Reasoning, arXiv:2504.13837

figures.caption: MATH-500 accuracy after 300 RLVR steps under different training signals (ground truth, majority vote, one-shot, format, incorrect label, random reward): weak and even spurious rewards produce large gains on Qwen models but not on Llama or OLMo.
figures.attribution: Figure from Shao et al. (2025), Spurious Rewards, arXiv:2506.10947

figures.caption: VinePPO credit-assignment example: for a sample chain-of-thought response, per-step estimated probability of correctness and Monte-Carlo advantages identify the single decisive reasoning step, alongside advantage distributions concentrated near zero for most tokens.
figures.attribution: Figure from Kazemnejad et al. (2024), VinePPO, arXiv:2410.01679

figures.caption: Taxonomy of task-specific RL for LLMs: reward source (rows) x optimization mechanism (columns), with per-cell paper counts and a cross-cutting root band.
figures.attribution: Original figure, this survey

figures.caption: Evolution of task-specific RL for LLMs, from pre-LLM execution-reward methods (Seq2SQL, 2017) through the GRPO/RLVR inflection to the 2025 domain explosion and reckoning.
figures.attribution: Original figure, this survey
